medications.drug_name: topical corticosteroid (e.g. hydrocortisone 1% or a mild/moderate steroid for face)
medications.indication_rationale: Reduces the superficial inflammation of a mild pemphigus foliaceus flare pending dermatology confirmation; use a face-appropriate potency.
Creates a protective barrier, relieves burning and prevents crust cracking.
These aggravate pemphigus foliaceus and must be stopped.
Systemic therapy for pemphigus is a specialist decision after confirmation, not a telehealth initiation.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stop all harsh shampoos/scrubbing, wash gently with lukewarm water, apply emollient then the steroid as directed, and don't pick the crusts.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Removes the aggravating factor and protects the skin.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like pemphigus foliaceus — an autoimmune condition where the top layer of skin loses its 'glue' and peels superficially. It's often mistaken for dandruff/seborrhoeic dermatitis, and harsh shampoos make it worse. It is NOT a flesh-eating or spreading infection.
Stop the anti-dandruff shampoos and harsh cleansers, wash gently with lukewarm water, apply plain petroleum jelly and the steroid cream, and don't pick the crusts.

See your GP within a few days for a dermatology referral and skin biopsy. Go to ED if you develop mouth/eye sores, clusters of small punched-out sores, a high fever, or thick yellow-green pus with rapidly spreading redness.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Response to topical therapy, dermatology referral for biopsy/immunofluorescence and long-term control.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Mucosal involvement, punched-out erosions/systemic illness, or spreading purulence — ED/urgent.

must_include_items: Exclude eczema herpeticum/SJS/impetigo (mucosal, punched-out erosions, fever, purulence) before reassuring
Topical corticosteroid + bland emollient and dermatology confirmation (biopsy)
errors_of_commission: ED over-triage of a superficial afebrile eruption as a 'flesh-eating infection'
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T2 case: passing requires the infection/mucosal screen + stopping aggravating products + topical therapy + dermatology confirmation. Missing herpeticum/SJS or ED over-triage are failures.
